name: Aho-Corasick multi-pattern matcher
task: Create /tmp/eval_ac/ac.py with class AhoCorasick(patterns) where patterns is a list of non-empty ASCII strings. Method find_all(text) returns a list of (start_index, pattern_index) tuples — every occurrence of every pattern in text — sorted by (start_index, pattern_index). Use suffix links (failure function) so the runtime is O(len(text) + total_matches), not O(len(text) * len(patterns)). The same pattern can appear multiple times; report each occurrence.

validation.script: import importlib.util; spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location('m', '/tmp/eval_ac/ac.py'); mod = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec); spec.loader.exec_module(mod); ac = mod.AhoCorasick(['he', 'she', 'his', 'hers']); r = ac.find_all('ahishers'); exp = sorted([(1,2),(3,1),(4,0),(4,3)]); assert sorted(r) == exp, f'got {sorted(r)}'; ac2 = mod.AhoCorasick(['ab', 'bc', 'abc', 'cd']); r2 = ac2.find_all('abcd'); exp2 = sorted([(0,0),(0,2),(1,1),(2,3)]); assert sorted(r2) == exp2, f'got {sorted(r2)}'; ac3 = mod.AhoCorasick(['aa']); r3 = ac3.find_all('aaaa'); assert sorted(r3) == [(0,0),(1,0),(2,0)], f'got {sorted(r3)}'; ac4 = mod.AhoCorasick(['a','ab','bab','bc','bca','c','caa']); r4 = ac4.find_all('abccab'); exp4 = sorted([(0,0),(0,1),(1,3),(2,5),(3,5),(4,0),(4,1)]); assert sorted(r4) == exp4, f'got {sorted(r4)}'; import time, random; random.seed(0); pats = [''.join(random.choice('abcde') for _ in range(random.randint(2,6))) for _ in range(50)]; pats = list(set(pats)); text = ''.join(random.choice('abcde') for _ in range(20000)); ac5 = mod.AhoCorasick(pats); start = time.time(); ac5.find_all(text); elapsed = time.time() - start; assert elapsed < 2.0, f'too slow: {elapsed:.2f}s — likely missing suffix links'; print('OK')
